exemplar.title: Investigating the effect of temperature on the resistivity of conductors

detailedExemplarData.title: Investigating the effect of temperature on the resistivity of conductors

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data recording and processing are communicated step-by-step with clear tables and a worked example
Processing of raw data (Râ calculation, resistivity derivation, RÂ² determination) is accurate
Initial uncertainty propagation for Râ is correctly applied
weaknesses: Uncertainties are not propagated to final resistivity values and error bars are missing on graphs
Systematic effects (e.g. cable resistance) are only discussed qualitatively later
Consideration of uncertainties has significant omissions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While data presentation and numerical processing are clear and accurate (band 5), the omission of uncertainty propagation to final results and lack of error bars limit the completeness of analysis. Evidence of uncertainty consideration is present but incomplete, placing the work in the 3â4 band. A holistic score of 3 reflects these significant omissions.

strengths: Clear framing of the research question within a specific global and personal engineering context
Detailed circuit diagram and apparatus description aiding reproducibility
Explicit statement of independent and dependent variables with stated uncertainties
weaknesses: Wire diameter is left as a placeholder and equilibration time is not specified
Number of repeats per temperature point is not defined
Minor methodological ambiguities (increment sizes implied rather than stated)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The contextualisation of the research question is strong and the method is clearly described with schematic support, placing it in the 3â4 band for reproducibility. However, small omissions (wire diameter, equilibration procedure, repeat measurements) prevent full explanation of methodological considerations required for the top band, so a holistic score of 4 is appropriate.

strengths: Conclusion is fully consistent with analysis, restating the linear relationship and quoting gradient with RÂ²
Comparison to Gilevâs literature data is justified and contextualised scientifically
weaknesses: Discussion could be slightly expanded to acknowledge minor deviations or uncertainty influence
No additional scientific contexts beyond the Gilev comparison are explored
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is justified and fully consistent with the analysis, and the scientific comparison to published data is relevant and well explained, satisfying the top descriptors. Minor extensions could reinforce robustness, but overall performance merits a 5.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses (thermometer resolution, stove control, cable resistance) are identified
Relevant and realistic improvements (digital thermometer, controlled bath, shorter cables) are proposed
weaknesses: Impact of identified weaknesses is not quantified
Improvements lack detailed implementation plans and expected effect magnitudes
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation moves beyond generic statements by naming specific limitations and realistic improvements, aligning with the 3â4 band. However, without quantification of impacts or detailed implementation, it does not reach the top of that band. A holistic score of 3 reflects this mid-band performance.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student frames the investigation around energyâloss problems in power transmission and computing, then narrows to copper wire in a laboratory set-up.  This links the research focus to both global and personal engineering contexts and makes clear why temperature dependence of resistivity matters.  Although the research question is not written as a single interrogative sentence, its scope ("how the temperature of conductors affects their resistivity") is explicitly stated and tightly connected to the described scenario, satisfying the descriptor for a specific and appropriate context.

Instrument uncertainties are stated, and propagation to Rx is shown with an explicit formula, yielding a table of absolute uncertainties.  However, uncertainties are not propagated to the final resistivity values, error bars are absent from the graph, and systematic effects (e.g., cable resistance) are only discussed qualitatively later.  This constitutes âevidence with some significant omissionsâ, matching the 3-4 band.

Key considerationsâinstrument uncertainties, temperature overshoot, cable resistance, choice of wide temperature rangeâare noted, showing awareness of relevance and sufficiency.  Nonetheless, the design collects only a single reading per temperature and omits justification for number of points or the absence of repeats, so the explanation of how the considerations secure adequate and reliable data remains limited rather than fully developed.

Specific weaknesses are identified (thermometer resolution, stove control, cable resistance) and their effects on precision or accuracy are described qualitatively.  While the student does not quantify the magnitude of each effect, the discussion moves beyond generic statements and addresses relative impact, placing the work in the 3-4 band.

The method specifies apparatus, thermometer resolution, range of temperatures, Wheatstone-bridge balancing procedure and formula used to compute resistance.  Photos and a circuit diagram further aid reproducibility.  However, the wire diameter is left as a placeholder, increments are only implied by the data table, and there is no mention of how long the system is allowed to equilibrate.  These minor ambiguities mean another experimenter could reproduce the work with only limited clarification, aligning with the 3-4 band.

All raw data are processed correctly: Rx is calculated using the bridge ratio, uncertainties are propagated appropriately, resistivity is derived with correct units, and the coefficient of determination (RÂ²) is computed.  No mathematical errors are evident, and results are consistent with accepted values.  Hence processing is both appropriate and accurate, meriting the top band.

Realistic improvementsâusing a digital thermometer, a controlled water bath, shorter cablesâare proposed and linked to how they would enhance precision or validity.  The explanations are present but relatively brief and lack detail on implementation or expected magnitude of improvement, aligning with âdescribedâ rather than fully âexplainedâ improvements.

Data tables include clear headings, units and uncertainties; processed values are displayed in a second table; a scatter graph with labelled axes and fitted line is provided.  Narrative text leads the reader step-by-step through the calculations.  Overall, the communication is simultaneously clear (easy to follow) and precise (numerical detail and correct symbols), fulfilling the top-band descriptor.

The student compares experimental values to literature data plotted from Gilev (2019) and notes the close agreement, explaining that this supports both accuracy and precision.  The comparison is relevant, explicitly justified and embedded in scientific context, satisfying the highest descriptor.

The conclusion restates the linear increase of resistivity with temperature, quotes the gradient, and directly references the plotted data and high RÂ².  The reasoning links back to the Drude model described earlier, demonstrating full consistency between analysis and conclusion and justifying the findings comprehensively.

annotations.comment: The title clearly states the investigation focus on temperature dependence of resistivity, providing an immediate overview of the studyâs scope.
The introduction adeptly links global energyâloss challenges and personal engineering experience, situating the research question in a specific and appropriate context.
Consider breaking the long introductory paragraph into smaller sections to improve readability and guide the reader through context, significance, and aim more clearly.
The description of the Drude model mentions free electrons in a sea but omits the electron symbol eâ»; include explicit notation to avoid ambiguity.
Equation (1) is correctly presented for resistivity, demonstrating accurate understanding of the Drude model.
The use of the MaxwellâBoltzmann formula for particle speed in gases warrants a caveat when applied to solids; add a sentence discussing this assumptionâs limitations.
The independent variable is clearly defined with its instrument and uncertainty stated (ÎT = Â±0.5 Â°C), enhancing methodological reproducibility.
The dependent variable and its uncertainties are thoroughly described, supporting accurate propagation of errors in subsequent analysis.
Control of wire diameter is implied but not specified; record the exact gauge or diameter value to ensure replicability.
The inclusion of a detailed circuit schematic for the Wheatstone bridge greatly aids reproducibility of the measurement setup.
The method omits the time allowed for the oil and wire to equilibrate at each temperature; specify an equilibrium period before recording data.
Raw data table clearly shows temperature, Râ, Iâ, Iâ, and calculated Râ with units, supporting transparent data recording.
The worked example calculating Râ at 23 Â°C is clearly presented step by step, enhancing clarity of data processing.
Include absolute uncertainties alongside Râ in Table 3 to reflect propagated errors and support uncertainty analysis.
The propagation of uncertainty formula (Eqn 5) is correctly applied, demonstrating proper consideration of measurement uncertainties.
Propagate uncertainties through to the final resistivity values and display error bars on the scatter plot to fully quantify data precision.
The scatter plot with a fitted line clearly illustrates the linear relationship between resistivity and temperature.
Stating the RÂ² value (0.9965) and interpreting it confirms the high degree of correlation and precision in the results.
The conclusion restates the observed linear increase of resistivity and directly references high RÂ², demonstrating full consistency with the analysis.
Comparing the experimental line with Gilevâs accepted data and discussing close agreement embeds findings in an appropriate scientific context.
The evaluation identifies the stove control issue and its qualitative impact on precision, showing understanding of specific methodological weakness.
Quantify the temperature overshoot errorâe.g., calculate typical ÎT during stove shutdownâto assess its magnitude on resistivity results.
The limitations table lists corrective actions but lacks detail on implementation and expected impact; expand each action with quantitative targets.
Identifying the wide temperature range as a strength highlights increased validity, effectively linking design to outcome quality.
Include repeat measurements at each temperature to assess random error and improve confidence in the resistivityâtemperature relationship.
While instructing to repeat measurements at different temperatures, the method does not specify repeating trials at each point; specify number of repeats to ensure statistical robustness.

rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
rubricData.name: Physics IA
